§6Research Reactor Fuels & Breeding§f

§aMaster the fuel types and optimize your breeding setup!§f

Plate fuel is made in a §bTier 4 Anvil§f using an ingot of the corresponding material. Neutron sources use billets instead. All fuels have two key stats: §bflux function§f (how output flux relates to input) and §byield§f (interactions before depletion). Radiation increases with depletion.

§6Neutron Sources§f:
§b• Ra226Be§f — constant flux §e30§f, yield §e1.3M§f. Starts at 3.75 RAD/s, ends at 22.5 RAD/s (Po-210 buildup). Recycling: 2 Be nuggets, 2 Po-210 nuggets, 1 coal powder, 1 lead nugget.
§b• Pu238Be§f — constant flux §e50§f (best source!), yield §e1M§f. Starts at 5 RAD/s, drops to 1 RAD/s (Pu-238 decay). Recycling: 1 Be nugget, 1 Pu-238 nugget, 2 coal powder, 2 lead nuggets.

§6Fuel Types§f:
§b• MOX§f — flux: log₁₀(x+1)·0.5·50. High yield §e2.4M§f. Starts 2.5 RAD/s, ends 187.5 RAD/s. Recycling: Sr-90, Pu-239, Cs-137, nuclear waste.
§b• U-235§f — flux: √x · 4.0. Yield §e2.2M§f. Starts 1 RAD/s, ends 100 RAD/s. Recycling: Np-237, Pu-238, Tc-99, nuclear waste.
§b• U-233§f — flux: √x · 5.0 (stronger than U-235). Yield §e2.2M§f. Starts 5 RAD/s, ends 500 RAD/s. Recycling: U-235, I-131, Sr-90, nuclear waste.
§b• Pu-239 (HEP)§f — flux: (x - x²/10000)/100 · 25. Very strong! Yield §e2M§f. Starts 5 RAD/s, ends 500 RAD/s. Best for advanced breeding. Recycling: Pu-240, Tc-99, Cs-137, nuclear waste.
§b• Sa-326 (HES)§f — flux: x/100 · 80 (extremely strong). Yield §e2M§f. Starts 15 RAD/s, ends 1,500 RAD/s! Recycling: Solinium, neodymium, tantalium, nuclear waste.

§6Breeding Mechanics§f:
• Only the §etotal flux value§f matters for breeder recipes
• Outer fuel plates receive less flux and deplete slower than inner plates
• Pu-239 HEP fuel has low critical mass — risk of runaway chain reaction!
• §c⚠§f Use a Reactor Remote Control Block or manual control rods to prevent meltdown with Pu-239

§eTip§f: Sa-326 is enormously radioactive but produces immense flux for the most demanding breeding recipes.
